definition:
1. to strip bare  of hair, (Herdotus Historicus):—; Pass. to become bald , (Hesiod): generally, to be laid bare , (Xenophon Historicus) 
2. with genitive to strip bare of, to strip of  a thing, (Herdotus Historicus) 
3. generally, to leave naked, unarmed  or defenceless , (Thucydides) 
4. Pass. also of things, to be stripped off  something, τὰ κρέα ἐψιλωμένα τῶν ὀστέων (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
